data.title: Entry-level crypto jobs will be scarce this year, warns Dragonfly. Job market was easier in 2021: Proof of Search’s Kevin Gibson.
data.description: According to Kevin Gibson from Proof of Search, the current job market shows significant changes compared to 2021, as obtaining entry-level positions has become more challenging.

data.title: Canada's PM diffused tensions in Washington with Trump, despite a few harsh remarks. Relations stabilized, but tense.
data.description: The Prime Minister of Canada visited Washington with the goal of improving the relationship between the two countries. His efforts were largely successful, at least temporarily.

data.title: Keynote speaker Dr. Tan See Leng to address at 18th SIEW with theme "Envisioning Energy Transformation."
data.description: Singapore Energy Lecture at the 18th Singapore International Energy Week (SIEW) on October 27, 2025 will be presented by Dr. Tan See Leng, Minister for Manpower and Minister-in-charge of Energy and Science & Technology. The event will focus on the theme "Envisioning..."

data.title: NACG will sell $125M of 7.75% senior unsecured notes due May 1, 2030 in reopening offer, with total issuance of $350M.
data.description: ACHESON, Alberta, October 7, 2025— North American Construction Group Ltd. (NACG) disclosed entering an underwriting agreement with a group of underwriters for a private placement offering. They plan to sell an additional $125 million in 7.75% senior unsecured notes due May 1, 2030, supplementing the $225 million already issued on May 1, 2025. The syndicate handling this includes National Bank Capital Markets, ATB Securities Inc., Scotia Capital Inc., TD Securities Inc., BMO Nesbitt Burns Inc., CIBC World Markets Inc., Canaccord Genuity Corp., and Raymond James Ltd. Post-closing, the combined principal amount of Initial Notes and Notes is projected to total $350 million.

data.title: S&P 500's winning streak ends, Dow Jones and major indexes drop, Nvidia retreats. Tesla's new price tag fails to boost shares.
data.description: On Tuesday, the Dow Jones average and other significant indexes experienced declines. Nvidia also saw a retreat in its stock value. The stock market today witnessed the S&P 500 ending its winning streak. Additionally, Tesla's new price tag failed to improve its share performance. This information was covered by Investor's Business Daily in live coverage.

data.title: Hiring nearly stopped in September with only 17,000 jobs created, compared to 54,000 predicted by economists.
data.description: According to exclusive data from the Carlyle Group, hiring came to a standstill in September, marking the lowest job growth since the 2020 recession recovery. As the government was shut down and official Bureau of Labor Statistics failed to provide figures, Carlyle Group filled the gap. The report indicated a mere 17,000 jobs were generated in September, far below the 54,000 forecasted by economists as reported by Reuters. This dismal figure follows the disappointing addition of 22,000 jobs in August. The complete report can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: GitHub updates CodeQL to 2.23.2, boosting Rust security with multi-language improvements, covering JS, Python, Ruby, and Go.
data.description: The recent update of GitHub's CodeQL to version 2.23.2 has brought improvements in security detection specifically for Rust, enhancing its accuracy. This update also includes enhancements for programming languages such as JavaScript, Python, Ruby, and Go.
